artifacts.summary: Official TU Graz discovery found central public AI guidance, a Rectorate guideline for AI-supported tools in teaching, a public AI-in-teaching page, and a Rectorate-created researcher guide. TU4U student and teacher pages redirected to SSO and were not bypassed. News, course, repository, and training/course pages were rejected or not used where more direct policy/guidance sources were available.

artifacts.title: Graz University of Technology AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official TU Graz public sources identify central AI guidance, a Rectorate teaching guideline, public teaching AI guidance, and a Rectorate researcher guide. The staged claims are guidance-scoped: TU Graz supports responsible AI use, lets teaching staff decide course/thesis AI-tool permissions, treats AI tools in examinations as unauthorized unless explicitly permitted, requires transparency/labelling where applicable, warns against entering sensitive data, encourages researchers to integrate AI responsibly, and provides Academic AI to employees. No broader central binding AI policy beyond these official guidance sources was found in public official sources.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
TU4U student and teacher pages redirected to Single Sign-On and were recorded as login-gated rather than accessed.
News, continuing-education course, and repository research results were rejected or not used for claims because direct official guidance sources were available.
The staged claims are not published canonical facts and require downstream review before public release.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
